extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Adam attacked 5km out on Vallter after Ineos forced rivals to chase via CARAPAZ bridging, dropping KUSS in final 1500m
tactical_patterns: Ineos used CARAPAZ as sacrificial domestique to mark early VALVERDE/QUINTANA moves, protecting YATES/THOMAS from energy-draining bridges despite being 52s down.
Jumbo and Quick-Step foolishly paced peloton to reduce 10min breakaway gap, helping Ineos with only Dennis/Castroviejo on flat before climb.
KUSS attacked YATES too early on flatter gradient 5km out, costing himself when dropped in steep final 1500m above 2000m where altitude should've suited him.
VALVERDE showed resurgent form with repeated accelerations 8km+ out, forcing tempo surges that weakened rivals before strong 3rd place finish.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: attacked when legs felt good rather than waiting for bonus seconds tactically, suggesting exceptional form beyond just GC calculation
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: relegated to marking duty for teammates despite climbing strength, multiple surges likely cost him individual result
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: negative: tactical error attacking on flatter gradient 5km out instead of waiting for steeper final 1500m above 2000m where altitude suits him
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: best climbing performance in years with repeated attacks 8km+ out, finished 19s back after forcing pace
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: 4th place with signature last-kilometer attack suggests Tour de France peak form returning
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: negative: dropped surprisingly early with 10km+ remaining on moderate gradients, significantly underperformed expectations
rider_signals.CHAVEZ Esteban: positive: closed 7-second gap to within 100m of Yates in final 2km before flattening terrain stopped momentum
